commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: This is not a safe automation lane because the remaining blockers are maintainer policy/security approval, live transport proof, and mergeability, not a narrow mechanical code defect.; Needs attention: The diff has no obvious supply-chain red flag, but it does add security-sensitive peer identity propagation into plugin tool context and delivery policy enforcement. (sha=a17488961068668ae0f46d328591b7774f4e596b)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds `session.sendPolicy.rules[].match.peerEquals: "inboundPeer"`, inbound-peer propagation, automatic-delivery suppression, explicit-send bypass/replay state, schema/docs/help metadata, and focused tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature/config API PR; source inspection confirms current main only supports static send-policy predicates, while the PR adds the requested relational predicate.
commands.review_followup: This is not a safe automation lane because the remaining blockers are maintainer policy/security approval, live transport proof, and mergeability, not a narrow mechanical code defect.

target.title: fix: add inbound peer send policy matching
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds native `session.sendPolicy.rules[].match.peerEquals: "inboundPeer"` support.
- Allows relational send-policy checks between the outbound target and the inbound peer for the current automatic-delivery turn, including `identityLinks` aliases.
- Threads inbound peer context through automatic replies, routed replies, tool context, plugin/gateway message-action context, and durable outbound delivery.
- Keeps send-policy-only predicates out of shared allow/deny scopes.
- Preserves explicit `message.send` compatibility by keeping explicit gateway/plugin/direct sends outside `session.sendPolicy` suppression.
- Reports blocked automatic durable sends as `denied_by_send_policy` suppression outcomes.
- Adds focused unit coverage and docs/help labels for the new predicate.

Fixes #85175.

## Verification

Latest head explicit-gateway-send follow-up validated locally: `fa5474b29b`.

Clean local worktree used: `~/projects/openclaw-pr-85763-send-policy`.

Commands run against latest head:

```text
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm exec vitest run src/sessions/send-policy.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts
pnpm exec vitest run src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts
node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/scripts/real-behavior-proof-policy.test.ts
```

Earlier broad validation on head `5b0f312431` before the schema-only follow-up:

```text
pnpm protocol:check
pnpm lint
pnpm check:test-types
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts
```

Observed local results on latest head:

```text
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile already up to date
src/sessions/send-policy.test.ts passed 13/13 tests
src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts passed 21/21 tests
test/scripts/real-behavior-proof-policy.test.ts passed 23/23 tests
```

Earlier broad validation results on head `5b0f312431`:

```text
pnpm protocol:check passed
pnpm lint passed with 0 warnings and 0 errors
pnpm check:test-types passed
src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts passed 39/39 tests
```

Earlier focused send-policy validation on this branch:

```text
pnpm test src/sessions/send-policy.test.ts src/config/schema.hints.test.ts src/cli/config-cli.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message.test.ts src/infra/outbound/outbound-send-service.test.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts
pnpm check:changed
```

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `sendPolicy` can now allow same-peer automatic delivery and suppress wrong-peer automatic outbound delivery when `peerEquals: "inboundPeer"` is configured.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux, clean worktree `~/projects/openclaw-pr-85763-send-policy`, branch `fix/send-policy-peer-equals-inbound-peer`, latest head `fa5474b29b`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: from the clean PR worktree, ran `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, ran the focused send-policy unit test, ran the gateway explicit-send regression test, and ran the real-behavior-proof policy test. Earlier on this branch, regenerated and checked protocol artifacts, ran lint, ran test type checks, ran the gateway startup regression test, ran focused send-policy shards, and ran `check:changed` before the final schema follow-up.

Evidence after fix:

Latest head terminal capture:

```text
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile already up to date
src/sessions/send-policy.test.ts passed 13/13 tests
test/scripts/real-behavior-proof-policy.test.ts passed 23/23 tests
```

Earlier broad validation terminal capture:

```text
pnpm protocol:check passed
pnpm lint passed with 0 warnings and 0 errors
pnpm check:test-types passed
src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts passed 39/39 tests
```

Earlier terminal capture from the focused send-policy run:

```text
[test] passed 5 Vitest shards in 32.58s
```

Earlier terminal capture from `pnpm check:changed`:

```text
[check:changed] typecheck core
[check:changed] typecheck core tests
[check:changed] lint core
runtime-sidecar-loaders: local runtime sidecar loaders look OK.
Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).
```

The tests cover same-peer allow, wrong-peer denial metadata, missing inbound peer behavior, `identityLinks` alias comparison, schema split rejection for shared scopes, inbound peer propagation, and automatic message-action send-policy suppression before transport.

Observed result after fix: same-peer configured automatic sends are allowed when the outbound target equals a trusted inbound peer candidate; wrong-peer automatic sends are denied/suppressed before transport with `send_policy_peer_mismatch` / `denied_by_send_policy` metadata. Explicit `message.send` compatibility is preserved by keeping explicit gateway/plugin/direct sends outside `session.sendPolicy` suppression.

What was not tested: no private Telegram credentials, IPs, phone numbers, API keys, or non-public endpoints are included here. Live Telegram proof has not been posted in this PR yet; if a maintainer or Mantis captures redacted Telegram proof, it can be appended as stronger external evidence.
